title: Drip irrigation system, method and controller
abstract: A drip irrigation system couples a pump, specifically a positive displacement pump, directly to a pipe network to deliver water to pressure compensating emitters. An optimum operating speed of the PD pump is determined by detecting dynamic response of pressure in the pipe network. That dynamic response may be detected from current response of the motor of the PD pump.
claims: Claims CLAIMS What is claimed is: 1. A drip irrigation system comprising: an irrigating fluid source; a field of pressure compensating emitters, each emitter controlling flow above an activation pressure; a pipe network delivering irrigating fluid from the fluid source to the pressure compensating emitters; a fluid pump that feeds irrigating fluid from the fluid source to the emitters through the pipe network; a controller that controls pump speed, the controller detecting dynamic response of pressure in the pipe network to determine an operating speed and controlling pump speed at that operating speed. 2. A method of drip irrigation comprising: pumping irrigating fluid from a fluid source to a field of pressure compensating emitters through a pipe network, each emitter controlling flow above an activation pressure; and controlling pump speed with a controller, the controller detecting dynamic response of pressure in the pipe network to determine an operating speed and controlling the pump speed at that operating speed. 3. A controller for controlling pump speed of a fluid pump that feeds fluid into a pipe network including a field of pressure compensating emitters, the controller detecting dynamic response of pressure in the pipe network to determine an operating speed and controlling the pump speed at that operating speed. 4. The drip irrigation system, method or controller as claimed in any of claims 1-3 wherein the fluid pump is a positive displacement pump. 5. The drip irrigation system, method or controller as claimed in claim 4 wherein the controller controls pump speed by controlling voltage input to a motor of the fluid pump, and dynamic response of pressure is detected from current response of the motor of the fluid pump. 6. The drip irrigation system, method or controller as claimed in claim 5 wherein the dynamic response of pressure is detected from gain of the current response of the motor of the fluid pump. 7. The drip irrigation system, method or controller as claimed in claim 6 wherein the controller increases voltage input to the motor of the fluid pump until a current spike is sensed and then reduces the voltage input to avoid the current spike. 8. The drip irrigation system as claimed in claim 7 wherein voltage input is reduced to a sufficiently low level to overcome hysteresis and then returned to a higher voltage level less than that at which the current spike occurs. 9. The drip irrigation system, method or controller as claimed in any of claims 5-8 wherein the voltage to the motor is increased in steps. 10. The drip irrigation system, method or controller as claimed in claim 6 wherein the dynamic response of pressure is detected from both gain and time constant of the current response of the motor of the fluid pump. 11. The drip irrigation system, method or controller as claimed in claim 10 wherein the operating speed is determined from an extremum of a cost function of gain and time constant of the current response of the motor of the fluid pump. 12. The drip irrigation system, method or controller as claimed in any of claims 1-11 wherein the detected dynamic response of pressure is a pressure spike in the pipe network. 13. The drip irrigation system, method or controller as claimed in any of claims 1-4 wherein the dynamic response of pressure is sensed with a pressure sensor in the pipe network. 14. The drip irrigation system, method or controller as claimed in any of claims 1-13 wherein the controller maintains pumping power at about a minimum required for all of the pressure compensating emitters to be at or above activation pressure.
